title: Source port reuse in rootless containers causes DNS lookup failure
body:
I am not certain if the fault lies with podman or elsewhere but it manifests both here and in buildah builds. Behavior observed on 3.x as well as `4.2.0`.

Logs below come from the host OS running `tcpdump [...]` to capture all DNS traffic, first running `nslookup github.com` directly and then inside a rootless container. 

`nslookup github.com` on the host:
 
```
10:48:28.596703 IP localhost.45391 > dns.53: 38490+ A? github.com. (28)
10:48:28.616989 IP dns.53 > localhost.45391: 38490 1/0/0 A $GH_V4_IP (44)
10:48:28.618189 IP localhost.54129 > dns.53: 40921+ AAAA? github.com. (28)
10:48:28.621230 IP dns.53 > localhost.54129: 40921 0/1/0 (112)
```

here we can see the v4 (success) and v6 (nxdomain) requests and responses getting routed like expected.

Now, from a `podman run --rm -it docker.io/library/alpine:latest`:

```
11:46:22.251801 IP localhost.53145 > dns.53: 40522+ A? github.com. (28)
11:46:22.251920 IP localhost.53145 > dns.53: 41702+ AAAA? github.com. (28)
11:46:22.256256 IP dnsserver.53 > localhost.53145: 40522 1/0/0 A $GH_V4_IP (44)
11:46:22.256257 IP dnsserver.53 > localhost.53145: 41702 0/1/0 (112)
```

As can be seen, the two requests use the same source port. Depending on the timing, this can cause the process to incorrectly interpret the v6 response for the v4 request or vice versa.

In our case, on some hosts this causes consistent failures in resolving `github.com`, resulting in build failures.


/kind bug


**Description**


**Output of `podman version`:**

`4.1.1` `4.2.0`, `3.4.7`


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Observed both on physical and qemu virtual hosts regardless of Linux distribution. Kernel versions ranging from `5.10` and `5.15`.

comments.body: Feels like more of a slirp4netns issue? Does this reproduce on root containers created with `--net slirp4netns`?

Hmmmmm, it does - but I also see it now on a root container with `--net=bridge` (should have tried that first eh). Not sure what's going on. Are you able to reproduce?

Also noting that even if I can replicate it reliably, it's only on Debian VMs (qemu KVM) where this has actually been causing issues. Not sure exactly what's up here.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
